item.label: Manual Mineral Input
item.description: Manual mineral targets for filter brewing based on common coffee-water guidance, with bottled-water catalog support when provenance and chemistry are sufficient.

caution.tooSoft: Water hardness is low. Expect less extraction power and consider a small temperature increase only after tasting.
caution.tooHard: Water hardness is high. Body may rise while clarity drops; avoid pushing temperature or contact time.
caution.tooLowAlkalinity: Low alkalinity can make acidity feel sharp. Keep agitation tidy before tightening grind.
caution.tooHighAlkalinity: High alkalinity can mute acidity and floral notes. Avoid compounding with very high temperature.

notes: Manual mineral entry remains the safest fallback, especially when market-SKU chemistry is incomplete.
TDS is total dissolved solids, while hardness and alkalinity should be entered separately when known.
Brand-water datasets include official, curated, and review-only records with confidence labels.
When a brand is flagged manual-required or review-only, prefer manual minerals over automatic trust.
